# preprocessing.outlier.get_outliers { #spotforecast2_safe.preprocessing.outlier.get_outliers }

```python
preprocessing.outlier.get_outliers(
    data,
    data_original=None,
    contamination=0.01,
    random_state=1234,
)
```

Detect outliers in each column using Isolation Forest.

This function uses scikit-learn's IsolationForest algorithm to detect outliers
in each column of the input DataFrame. The original data (before any NaN values
were introduced) can be provided to identify which values were marked as NaN due
to outlier detection.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name          | Type                                                                            | Description                                                                                                                                      | Default    |
|---------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| data          | [pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`)                                  | The input DataFrame to check for outliers.                                                                                                       | _required_ |
| data_original | [Optional](`typing.Optional`)\[[pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`)\] | Optional original DataFrame before outlier marking. If provided, helps identify which values became NaN due to outlier detection. Default: None. | `None`     |
| contamination | [float](`float`)                                                                | The estimated proportion of outliers in the dataset. Default: 0.01.                                                                              | `0.01`     |
| random_state  | [int](`int`)                                                                    | Random seed for reproducibility. Default: 1234.                                                                                                  | `1234`     |

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type                                                                            | Description                                                    |
|--------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------|
|        | [Dict](`typing.Dict`)\[[str](`str`), [pd](`pandas`).[Series](`pandas.Series`)\] | A dictionary mapping column names to Series of outlier values. |
|        | [Dict](`typing.Dict`)\[[str](`str`), [pd](`pandas`).[Series](`pandas.Series`)\] | For columns without outliers, an empty Series is returned.     |

## Raises {.doc-section .doc-section-raises}

| Name   | Type                       | Description                              |
|--------|----------------------------|------------------------------------------|
|        | [ValueError](`ValueError`) | If data is empty or contains no columns. |

## Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

```python
>>> import pandas as pd
>>> import numpy as np
>>> from spotforecast2_safe.preprocessing.outlier import get_outliers
>>>
>>> # Create sample data with outliers
>>> np.random.seed(42)
>>> data = pd.DataFrame({
...     'A': np.concatenate([np.random.normal(0, 1, 100), [10, 11, 12]]),
...     'B': np.concatenate([np.random.normal(5, 2, 100), [100, 110, 120]])
... })
>>> data_original = data.copy()
>>>
>>> # Detect outliers
>>> outliers = get_outliers(data_original, contamination=0.03)
>>> for col, outlier_vals in outliers.items():
...     print(f"{col}: {len(outlier_vals)} outliers detected")
```
